anti_patterns_ref.statement: Do not treat change-log or descriptive prose as prescriptive translation guidance.
Harmonize keys only — never rewrite translated text. A harmonize task that requires text changes is mislabeled; stop and escalate.

context: Brand names stay in English across all locales; only surrounding grammar adapts.
[SHOULD] Prefer concise phrasing where meaning is preserved, and respect component character limits.
[SHOULD] Use active imperative voice for action labels and declarative voice for status and error messages.

examples.note: Infinitive 'Brûler' for the button label, per rule.fr-infinitive-buttons.
examples.source: Burn this secret before it is read.
examples.target: Brûler ce secret avant qu'il ne soit lu.

examples.note: Uses 'Détruire' instead of the glossary term 'brûler' — terminology drift.
examples.source: Burn this secret before it is read.
examples.target: Détruire ce secret avant qu'il ne soit lu.

examples.note: Uses the European 'e-mail' instead of the Canadian 'courriel' — violates rule.fr_CA-courriel.

examples.source: This message is encrypted with your passphrase.
examples.target: Ce message est chiffré avec votre phrase secrète.

examples.note: Uses 'crypté' (deprecated/incorrect in security French) instead of 'chiffré', plus informal 'ta'.
examples.source: This message is encrypted with your passphrase.
examples.target: Ce message est crypté avec ta phrase secrète.

examples.source: Copy the secret link to share it.
examples.target: Copiez le lien secret pour le partager.

examples.note: Informal imperative 'Copie' plus 'ton' — forbidden tutoiement register.
examples.source: Copy the secret link to share it.
examples.target: Copie ton lien secret pour le partager.

examples.source: Enter the passphrase to view this secret.
examples.target: Saisissez la phrase secrète pour consulter ce secret.

examples.note: Uses 'mot de passe' (login credential) where 'phrase secrète' (secret protection) is meant — violates rule.fr-passphrase-password.
examples.source: Enter the passphrase to view this secret.
examples.target: Saisissez le mot de passe pour consulter ce secret.

examples.source: Enter your password to sign in.
examples.target: Saisissez votre mot de passe pour vous connecter.

examples.note: Informal imperative 'Saisis' plus 'ton' and 'te' — forbidden tutoiement register.
examples.source: Enter your password to sign in.
examples.target: Saisis ton mot de passe pour te connecter.

examples.note: Informal possessive 'Ton' — the forbidden tutoiement register.

rules.statement: Use the locale's locked register form (formality, pronoun, possessives) in all UI and email content.
Keep web.auth.security.* messages generic — never reveal which credential failed, precise timing, attempt counts, or account existence.
Preserve interpolation placeholders ({var}, {0}, %{var}) exactly — identical names, count, and syntax as the source string.
Express countable strings with vue-i18n pipe syntax so the target language's plural forms are expressible.
Do not translate or transliterate brand names (Onetime Secret, One-Time Secret, Identity Plus, Starlight); adapt only the surrounding grammar.
Do not translate underscore-prefixed metadata keys (_README, _meta, _translation_guidelines); they are documentation and never user-facing.
Use the glossary's chosen term for each concept consistently; do not vary terminology across strings for the same sense.
Destructive or irreversible action labels name their object (Delete Account, not Delete).
French content uses the formal vous register (vous / votre / vos) throughout product UI and email content; informal tutoiement (tu / ton / ta / tes) is forbidden.
Place a non-breaking space before the double punctuation marks `:` `;` `!` `?`, per French typographic convention.
Use the infinitive for button and link labels (Mettre à niveau), and the noun form for titles and headings (Mise à niveau).
Keep mot de passe (system authentication / login) distinct from phrase secrète (protection of a secret); never use one where the other is meant.
Canadian French uses "courriel" for email throughout UI and email content; the European "e-mail" form is not used in fr_CA.
